notes: Equipment/fixture symbol on '06-시설물-설비' layer built from arcs, circles and short tick lines; small ~109x80 unit footprint typical of a mechanical/plumbing symbol, not a wall.

The few LINE entities are very short (under 1.2 units) and clustered near the origin, consistent with leader ticks or connector stubs, not structural wall segments. The bounding box (~109 x 80 units) is small and irregular, matching a single equipment symbol footprint rather than a room or wall run.
rationale.rule: When a block's entities sit entirely on a layer whose name explicitly denotes equipment/facility (설비/시설물) and the primitive mix is arc/circle-heavy with only short line stubs, classify as a symbol rather than a wall or furniture item, since walls are dominated by long straight parallel LINE/LWPOLYLINE pairs.
rationale.counterfactual: If the layer histogram instead showed a wall-naming convention (e.g. WAL/A-WALL) or the LINE entities were long, parallel, and offset by a constant wall thickness, wall_likelihood would rise sharply.
rationale.uncertainty: Without rendered DWG geometry the exact fixture type (e.g. floor drain vs. valve) cannot be confirmed from histogram and handle data alone, though this does not affect the wall/symbol classification.

rationale.counterfactual: If the block instead contained a pair of long parallel LINEs spanning the full bbox width at a fixed offset (a wall-thickness pattern), it would be reclassified toward a wall/partition fragment.

rationale.counterfactual: If the WID layer name were absent and only the two 100-unit-offset parallel lines were visible without jamb/glazing detail, this would be indistinguishable from an actual short wall segment; the layer name and internal frame/glazing detail are what resolve it to a window.
rationale.uncertainty: The exact wall thickness this window is designed for elsewhere in the drawing (whether it is always 100 units) cannot be confirmed from this isolated block definition.
